description: Klearly powers B2B revenue teams with the visibility and confidence to know where, when, and how to grow revenue.

build_patterns.name: Agentic Architectures
evidence: AI agents deployed by the Ministry of Health to automate common requests, guide users, and improve access to healthcare services.
Our conversational agents go far beyond a simple chatbot and provide immediate, personalized assistance across all your channels.
For situations that require expert intervention, the conversation can be seamlessly transferred to a human advisor (livechat) in a single step, with no disruption for the user.
The AI agent detects when expert support is required and routes the request to live chat form or priority call with all contextual data handed over to the advisor.
Omnichannel AI Agent
Augmented Advisor and Mailbot
build_patterns.description: Klearly (DialOnce) implements agentic architectures through autonomous AI agents capable of multi-step reasoning, tool use (e.g., routing to live agents, integrating with CCaaS/CRM), and orchestrating customer journeys across channels.

build_patterns.name: Continuous-learning Flywheels
evidence: combination of NLU with generative AI, and continuous training, DialOnce’s conversational agents and chatbots achieve best-in-class intent understanding rates
Thanks to disambiguation mechanisms, the combination of NLU with generative AI, and continuous training
Measurable performance: your KPIs in real time
build_patterns.description: The system leverages continuous training and feedback loops to improve intent recognition and agent performance, using real-time KPIs and customer interactions to refine models.

build_patterns.name: Vertical Data Moats
evidence: Our proprietary AI model is built on in-depth knowledge of sector-specific contact reasons, backed by more than 150 successful projects.
DialOnce has been developing AI agents and enhanced chatbots tailored to each industry, capable of understanding a wide range of customer intents and adapting responses to the specific context.
Developed and hosted in France
France’s leading AI agent provider, built on 10 years of customer-service expertise and powered by French, sovereign technology.
build_patterns.description: Klearly/DialOnce leverages proprietary, industry-specific datasets and deep domain expertise in sectors like banking, insurance, and transport to create a defensible data moat.

build_patterns.name: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
evidence: Agent Assist: streamlined access to information and answer recommendations based on knowledge and collected context
Mailbot: automatic classification of incoming emails (including attachments) and answer generation
Integrated into your ecosystem (Kiamo, Genesys, Salesforce…) discover our three add-ons
build_patterns.description: The platform hints at retrieval-augmented generation by surfacing context-aware answers and recommendations for agents, likely combining retrieval from knowledge bases with generative responses.

build_patterns.name: Micro-model Meshes
evidence: multiple specialized solutions: Agent Assist, Mailbot, Automatic Call Summary
Solutions (virtual assistant, mailbot, automated post-call summary...) to automate your advisors' tasks
build_patterns.description: The use of distinct, task-specific AI modules (e.g., mailbot, call summary, agent assist) suggests a mesh of micro-models optimized for different customer service functions.

sub_vertical: B2B revenue intelligence and sales analytics

signals: Presence of a documentation section and content library
Multiple case studies and white papers indicating some technical depth
References to integrations with platforms like Salesforce, Genesys, Microsoft, etc.
Technical resources such as ebooks and trends articles

unique_findings: Klearly (DialOnce) leverages a sovereign, France-hosted AI infrastructure, emphasizing data residency and compliance—a rare technical choice in global AI agent deployments, especially for regulated industries.

The platform integrates Visual IVR to redirect inbound phone calls to digital channels (chatbot, AI agent, FAQ, etc.), blending telephony and digital customer service journeys in real time. This hybrid orchestration is technically complex and not commonly seen in most AI agent platforms.

Their AI agent architecture combines generative AI with proprietary NLU and continuous disambiguation mechanisms, focusing on high intent understanding rates and sector-specific adaptation. This layered approach goes beyond standard chatbot frameworks.

Deep integrations with CCaaS (Contact Center as a Service) and CRM systems (Salesforce, Genesys, Kiamo, etc.) enable automated post-call summaries, mail classification, and advisor assist features—indicating hidden complexity in workflow automation and context sharing across channels.

The system is designed for omnichannel orchestration, maintaining context and seamless handoff between digital and voice channels, including live advisor escalation with full context transfer. This cross-channel context persistence is technically challenging and rare.

Performance claims (90% satisfaction, 15% call avoidance, +30 reachability points, +20% productivity) are backed by real-time KPI dashboards, suggesting robust analytics and feedback loops built into the platform.

story_angles.headline: Inside Klearly’s Agentic Mesh: How Micro-Models and RAG Power Omnichannel AI
story_angles.summary: Klearly’s architecture leverages a mesh of micro-models and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) to deliver omnichannel AI agents that outperform traditional chatbots. This approach enables rapid adaptation to domain-specific needs, seamless context handoff across channels, and robust intent disambiguation, setting a new bar for customer service automation.
evidence: Build Patterns Detected: Agentic Architectures, Micro-model Meshes, RAG
Key features include seamless channel orchestration, context sharing, and advanced NLU with generative AI

story_angles.headline: Klearly’s Vertical Data Moats: The Real AI Edge in Customer Service
story_angles.summary: Rather than relying solely on generic LLMs, Klearly builds proprietary vertical data moats by deeply integrating with sector-specific workflows and contact reasons. This enables their AI agents to deliver highly relevant, context-aware responses that generic chatbots simply can’t match.
evidence: Proprietary AI model built on in-depth knowledge of sector-specific contact reasons
More than 150 successful projects across banking, insurance, transport, and public services

story_angles.headline: Why Deep Domain Expertise Still Wins: Klearly’s 10-Year Playbook for AI in Regulated Industries
story_angles.summary: Klearly’s decade-long focus on regulated and complex verticals—like banking, insurance, and public services—has resulted in AI agents that understand nuanced customer intents and compliance requirements. Their track record shows that vertical expertise, not just tech, is the key to real-world AI adoption.
evidence: Trusted by demanding professionals for over 10 years in regulated sectors
Proprietary models tuned to industry-specific workflows and compliance

story_angles.headline: Why Klearly Chose Sovereign, France-Hosted AI Over Big Cloud LLMs
story_angles.summary: In a world racing to the cloud, Klearly doubled down on sovereign, France-hosted AI infrastructure—prioritizing security, data residency, and regulatory alignment. This contrarian bet appeals to privacy-conscious enterprises and sets Klearly apart from US-centric SaaS competitors.
evidence: Solution is sovereign (hosted in France), secure, and transparent
Emphasis on responsible, high-performance AI for regulated European markets

anti_patterns.description: The marketing is heavy on buzzwords such as 'AI-powered', 'generative AI', 'sovereign AI', and 'next-generation agent', but lacks technical specifics about the underlying models, architectures, or proprietary technology. There is frequent mention of 'intent understanding' and 'omnichannel orchestration', but no concrete details on how these are achieved or what differentiates their approach from standard LLM integrations.
evidence: Claims of 'best-in-class intent understanding rates' without technical substantiation.
Repeated use of terms like 'generative AI', 'proprietary AI model', and 'sovereign technology' without any mention of model architecture, training data, or unique algorithms.
No mention of specific LLMs, technical stack, or RAG/micro-model mesh implementation details.

anti_patterns.description: While the company claims a 'vertical data moat' and industry-specific expertise, there is little evidence of a defensible data advantage or technical differentiation. The platform appears to be a service layer integrating with existing CCaaS/CRM systems and leveraging generic AI capabilities, which could be replicated by competitors or incumbents with access to similar APIs and integration partners.
evidence: No clear description of proprietary data assets or unique datasets.
Integration with standard platforms (Salesforce, Genesys, etc.) is common among competitors.
Claims of '150 successful projects' and '10 years of expertise' are not backed by evidence of a technical moat (e.g., unique models, exclusive data).

anti_patterns.description: Many of the highlighted capabilities (Visual IVR, mailbot, agent assist, post-call summary) are features that could be absorbed by larger platforms or added by incumbents. The product positioning seems to rely on bundling these features rather than presenting a platform with a clear, defensible scope.
evidence: Features like mailbot, agent assist, and call summary are increasingly standard in CCaaS and CRM platforms.
No evidence of a broader platform vision beyond incremental feature additions.
Emphasis on integrations rather than core platform innovation.

anti_patterns.description: The offering is positioned as a 'next-generation AI agent' but lacks a unique angle in a crowded market. The focus on omnichannel orchestration and customer service automation is common among many vendors, and there is little to suggest a distinctive approach or technology.
evidence: No clear differentiation from other AI agent providers in terms of technology, data, or UX.
Heavy reliance on industry buzzwords and standard integrations.

competitors.name: Clari
competitors.similarity: Both provide revenue operations and analytics platforms for B2B sales and marketing teams, focusing on pipeline visibility and forecasting.
competitors.how_different: Klearly emphasizes actionable insights for where, when, and how to grow revenue, with a focus on marketing and sales alignment, while Clari is more focused on sales forecasting and pipeline management.

competitors.name: Gong
competitors.similarity: Both use AI to analyze sales and revenue team activities, aiming to improve pipeline performance and revenue outcomes.
competitors.how_different: Gong is conversation intelligence-centric, analyzing calls and emails, while Klearly focuses more broadly on revenue team actions and marketing/sales data to drive growth recommendations.

competitors.name: People.ai
competitors.similarity: Both aggregate and analyze sales and marketing activity data to provide revenue intelligence and actionable insights.
competitors.how_different: People.ai is heavily focused on activity capture and CRM enrichment, while Klearly positions itself as providing confidence and visibility into where and how to grow revenue, with a more prescriptive approach.

competitors.name: InsightSquared
competitors.similarity: Both offer revenue analytics, forecasting, and pipeline management for B2B teams.
competitors.how_different: InsightSquared emphasizes analytics and reporting, while Klearly highlights actionable recommendations and growth prioritization.

differentiation.primary: Klearly differentiates by providing B2B revenue teams with actionable, prescriptive insights on where, when, and how to grow revenue, rather than just analytics or reporting.
differentiation.technical: Klearly leverages machine learning to synthesize marketing and sales data, focusing on actionable recommendations and confidence scoring for revenue growth opportunities.
differentiation.business: Klearly targets both marketing and sales teams, positioning itself as a unifying platform for revenue teams (not just sales or marketing), with a SaaS model and integrations into existing workflows.
differentiation.positioning: Klearly positions itself as the platform that gives revenue teams the visibility and confidence to act, bridging the gap between marketing and sales, and moving beyond reporting to prescriptive guidance.

secret_sauce.core_advantage: Klearly’s unique advantage is its focus on actionable, prescriptive insights that unify marketing and sales data, enabling revenue teams to know exactly where, when, and how to act for growth.
secret_sauce.defensibility: This is defensible due to their proprietary machine learning models, integration of multiple data sources, and focus on actionable recommendations (not just analytics). Their approach to aligning marketing and sales with a single source of truth is difficult to replicate without deep domain expertise and data integration.
evidence: Company description: 'Klearly powers B2B revenue teams with the visibility and confidence to know where, when, and how to grow revenue.'
Emphasis on actionable insights and prescriptive recommendations throughout platform messaging.
Positioning as a unifying platform for both marketing and sales teams.

competitive_analysis.moat_explanation: Klearly’s competitive position is defensible due to its proprietary machine learning models, actionable recommendations, and focus on unifying marketing and sales data. However, the space is competitive with well-funded incumbents (Clari, Gong, People.ai) who could build similar features. Continued differentiation will depend on execution, data integrations, and maintaining a lead in prescriptive guidance.

evidence_quotes: Powered by generative AI, they accurately understand natural language and enable your users to find answers autonomously (self-care), reducing the number of requests directed to your support teams.
Our conversational agents go far beyond a simple chatbot and provide immediate, personalized assistance across all your channels.
Agent Assist: streamlined access to information and answer recommendations based on knowledge and collected context
Mailbot: automatic classification of incoming emails (including attachments) and answer generation
Automatic Call Summary: instant post-call summary generation.

Thanks to disambiguation mechanisms, the combination of NLU with generative AI, and continuous training, DialOnce’s conversational agents and chatbots achieve best-in-class intent understanding rates, significantly outperforming a traditional chatbot.
Ebook - Generative AI Chatbot and Customer Service
Our proprietary AI model is built on in-depth knowledge of sector-specific contact reasons, backed by more than 150 successful projects.
Sovereign AI hosting: Emphasis on data residency and sovereignty (hosted in France) as a technical differentiator.
Omnichannel orchestration: Seamless context-sharing and handoff between channels (voice, chat, IVR, etc.) with full context transfer.
Visual IVR: Digitizing inbound calls and redirecting to digital channels, blending telephony with AI-driven digital journeys.
